questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning

questions.stem: Antonym of ARDENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The automobile industry is shifting towards electric vehicles. Q: Battery technology has improved significantly in recent years. R: Charging infrastructure is being expanded rapidly. S: This transition will reduce carbon emissions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of AUDACIOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Climate change is one of the biggest challenges today. Q: Rising temperatures cause extreme weather events. R: Governments worldwide are taking mitigation measures. S: However, collective action at individual level is also needed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ABUNDANT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Natural disasters cause widespread destruction. Q: Early warning systems can reduce the impact. R: Preparedness and planning are essential. S: Governments invest in disaster management infrastructure.

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coal is formed over millions of years. Q: Under heat and pressure, peat transforms into coal. R: Dead plant material accumulates in swamps forming peat. S: This process is called coalification.

questions.stem: Antonym of HARMONY:

questions.stem: Antonym of EPHEMERAL:

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Artificial intelligence is transforming industries. Q: Machine learning algorithms analyse vast amounts of data. R: AI-powered systems can make predictions and recommendations. S: Ethical considerations around AI are being debated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fatigue life of a bearing is expressed as:
options: L₁₀ life in millions of revolutions
questions.solution: L₁₀: 90% survive this many million revolutions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ton of refrigeration (TR) is:
options: The cooling rate of 1 ton of ice melting in 24 hours (3.517 kW)
questions.solution: 1 TR = 3.517 kW = 12000 BTU/hr. 1 ton ice = 2000 lb, latent heat 144 BTU/lb.

questions.solution: Kirchhoff: absorptivity = emissivity at given T and λ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The purpose of a key in a shaft-hub joint is to:
questions.solution: Key transmits torque between shaft and hub.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delay period in CI engines is:
options: Time between injection start and ignition
questions.solution: Delay period: physical+chemical delay before ignition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Carnot engine takes 1000 kJ from a source at 800 K and rejects to 300 K. The work output is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The rake angle of a cutting tool:
options: Angle between tool face and normal to work surface (affects chip flow)
Angle between tool flank and work
questions.solution: Positive rake: easier cutting (lower forces). Negative rake: stronger edge (for hard materials).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hardness of a material is measured by:
questions.solution: Hardness: Brinell (HB), Rockwell (HRC, HRB), Vickers (HV). Related to wear resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A four-bar linkage has:
questions.solution: Four-bar: 4 links (1 fixed = frame, 2 cranks/rockers, 1 coupler).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a screw jack, the effort required to lift a load W is:
questions.solution: Effort = W tan(α + φ) for square threads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Belt drives transmit power by:
questions.solution: Belt: flat, V-belt, timing belt. V-belt wedge action = higher friction. Slip = power loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The air-standard cycle for a spark-ignition (petrol) engine is the:
questions.solution: SI engines approximate the constant-volume heat-addition Otto cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Enthalpy h is defined as:
questions.solution: Enthalpy h = u + pv, a convenient property for flow processes.

options: Austenite grain size and alloying elements

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A governor controls:
options: Mean speed of an engine (regulates fuel supply)
questions.solution: Governor: centrifugal (Watt, Porter, Proell) or inertia type. Maintains constant speed under varying load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Percentage elongation:
questions.solution: Ductile: >5% elongation. Brittle: <5%. Copper: 45%. Cast iron: <1%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Relative humidity is:
options: The ratio of water to air
The ratio of actual vapor pressure to saturation vapor pressure at same temperature
questions.solution: RH = P_v/P_sat × 100%. RH 100% = saturated air (fog, rain).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The function of a governor is to:
options: Keep the mean speed nearly constant under varying load
questions.solution: A governor adjusts fuel/working-fluid supply to maintain mean speed as load changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The wet bulb temperature is:
options: The temperature air reaches when cooled evaporatively to saturation
questions.solution: WB: measured by sling psychrometer. DB-WB difference indicates dryness.

questions.solution: Addendum: radial height above pitch circle.

questions.stem: Adding a fin to a surface improves heat dissipation only when the fin's:
questions.solution: A fin helps only if it transfers more heat than the bare base area it covers (effectiveness > 1).

Maximum stress for infinite fatigue life

questions.stem: On the tensile stress–strain curve shown for mild steel (yield ≈ 250 MPa), point A (end of the initial straight line) represents the:
questions.solution: Point A is the yield point where the linear elastic region ends; the peak (B) is the ultimate strength.

options: A series of meshed gears to change speed or torque
questions.solution: Train value = product of driver teeth / product of driven teeth = N_out/N_in.

questions.solution: Ductile materials (e.g. mild steel) show significant elongation before failure.
